rationale: Neither candidate recovers the ground truth's actual substantive suggestion (dsocek's packed_modules_mapping-driven naming-conflict idea, likely tied to a hunk removed before merge per tthakkal's reply), so recall is low and roughly equal for both, matched mainly by the implicit LGTM/COMMENT-level verdict. Both converge on a similar valid but GT-unmatched theme (derive component list from _dit_modules instead of hardcoding), but Y grounds its claims more thoroughly with explicit repo-wide greps (consumer sweep, registry.py divergence, MagiHuman's missed sr_dit, other self.unet references), giving it an edge in precision. X contributes one genuinely distinct and useful insight (text-encoder LoRA still silently no-ops, plus a systemic 'warn on zero-match adapter' suggestion) that Y lacks, keeping actionability close between the two.
